title: Harden Claude executable identity for current packaged binaries
goal: Replace the obsolete 64 MiB in-memory Claude executable identity gate with bounded regular-file validation and streamed hashing that safely supports the current packaged Claude binary without starting a provider process.

approval.note: Provider use is forbidden. Repair and prove executable identity handling synthetically and against the installed binary only.

acceptance.assertion: Claude identity resolves one executable regular file, rejects empty, special, changing or unreasonably large files, and records exact size and resolved path.
SHA-256 is computed in bounded chunks without reading the entire executable into memory; a before/after stat identity check detects replacement or mutation during hashing.
A provider-free exact-path test accepts and hashes the installed 259,820,344-byte Claude 2.1.208 executable and matches an independently computed SHA-256.
Tests cover empty, oversized, non-regular, changed-during-read and ordinary small executable cases plus the complete Grabowski suite and CI.
No provider process, model token or provider cost is produced; this task does not authorize T002F retry or any replacement live pair.

metadata.outcome: Grabowski PR #259 replaced the obsolete 64 MiB in-memory Claude executable identity gate with regular-file validation, the runner-owned 512 MiB ceiling, 1 MiB streamed SHA-256 hashing and before/after file-identity checks. The originally triggering Claude 2.1.208 binary was proven provider-free at 259,820,344 bytes. Current-main revalidation on 2026-07-22 also passed against installed Claude 2.1.217 at 268,573,680 bytes. No provider process was started and no live-pair authorization is created.

verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002g-regular-file-boundary: PR #259 resolves one executable regular file, rejects empty, non-regular, non-executable and oversized inputs, and retains resolved path plus exact byte count.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002g-streamed-hash: PR #259 hashes in fixed 1 MiB chunks under the runner-owned 512 MiB ceiling and compares device, inode, mode, size, mtime and ctime before and after hashing; focused tests detect in-place mutation and path replacement.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002g-current-binary: The PR evidence records provider-free identity for Claude 2.1.208 at 259,820,344 bytes with SHA-256 125372839bc827ca24dd72382627b291fbca615408d732fe3291bc16723ce7f3. Current closeout independently revalidated the same contract against installed Claude 2.1.217 at 268,573,680 bytes.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002g-regression: PR evidence records 9 focused identity tests plus the complete preflight file, 1,970-test full repository suite, make validate and make deploy-check passing; required GitHub validate and CodeQL checks also passed. Current main additionally passes 39 focused preflight tests with only the opt-in installed-binary test skipped in the default run, then that opt-in test passes explicitly.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002g-no-provider: PR evidence and current verification are provider-free: provider_processes_started=0 and observed_cost_usd=0.0. T002H remains separately unauthorized.

self_review.scope: Full GitHub diff of Grabowski PR #259 plus current-main execution-binding readback.
findings: No blocking finding: regular-file, non-empty, executable and runner-owned 512 MiB bounds are fail-closed.
SHA-256 is streamed in fixed 1 MiB chunks and file identity is checked before and after hashing to detect mutation or replacement.
Live execution is separately bound to an absolute Claude executable path and expected SHA-256; the runner validates that binding before provider start and again after execution.
The opt-in installed-binary identity test is provider-free and independently recomputes the streamed digest.
self_review.external_review_status: not_counted: the PR contains only a Codex usage-limit notice and no completed external code review.
